5_content_quality.5a_notes: Creator View cites _transcripts.py TranscriptList.build(), _errors.py (RequestBlocked.cause, CAUSE_MESSAGE), formatters.py (_seconds_to_timestamp), proxies.py (WebshareProxyConfig, GenericProxyConfig), and README IP ban section across sections 1, 2, and 6. Well over the 2-citation bar.
5_content_quality.5b_notes: Conversational analyst voice (e.g., 'It's the difference between OCR-ing a printed page and just reading it.'). Paragraph flow with connective prose; Section 3 uses a comparison table appropriately, not as compliance scaffolding.
5_content_quality.5c_notes: Section 2 references home projects (T27, JASON-OS) and generic stack descriptors (npm workspace, TypeScript strict) but names no specific SoNash files/scripts/features to verify. Acceptable for external-library analysis, recorded as WARN per spec.

6_extractions.notes: value-map.json lists 11 candidates but analysis.json.candidates and extraction-journal.jsonl both contain only 10. Missing candidate: '100% coverage + 2-dep library packaging' (T2 pattern) — present in value-map.json and creator-view.md Section 5 T2 table, absent from analysis.json and journal. No explicit 'skipped/rejected' disposition recorded.

7_cross_file.7a_notes: research-index.jsonl contains research-topic entries only (15 rows, all L1/L2/L3 research topics); no per-analysis slug entries exist for any source. Cannot verify depth match — not a silent-drift failure for this source.
7_cross_file.7b_notes: analysis.json semantic_tags=[repo,library,youtube,transcript,repository]. value-map and journal candidate tags are uniformly [repo,library] — much narrower than analysis-level tags. Signal of under-tagged candidates, not a hard gate.
